title: Magnon bandstructure and topology in a periodically deformed Kagome lattice with DM interaction
authors: Mohammed Abdullah Hammadi, Mohammed Salman Alsadah, Husam Abdulmajeed Noorwli, Hocine Bahlouli, Michael Vogl
abstract: We study the band structure and topology of magnons for a Heisenberg model with DM interaction on a deformed Kagome lattice. For simplicity, we focus on a periodically deformed lattice with hexagonal symmetry and an enlarged unit cell. This enlarged unit cell gives rise to a richer band structure than in the undeformed case. Analyzing band topology, there is a distinction between the topologically trivial case with anti-ferromagnetic coupling and the topologically rich case with ferromagnetic coupling. In the anti-ferromagnetic case, a spin-space symmetry, also present in the classical ground state, enforces these topologically trivial states. In the ferromagnetic case, this symmetry is spontaneously broken by the classical ground state. Consequently, the band structure also hosts rich topological features. Specifically, we observe many topological transitions and bands with Chern numbers ranging from $+2$ to $-2$, which makes the system richer than in the undeformed case. This emergent rich topological structure demonstrates that deformed magnets can host new and exciting physics.
summary: This work theoretically examines how periodic deformations on a Kagome lattice modify the magnon band structure when DM interactions are present. By comparing anti-ferromagnetic and ferromagnetic couplings, the authors show that deformation can induce rich topological features, evidenced by large Chern numbers. This highlights lattice engineering as a key tool for realizing novel topological physics in magnetic materials.
main_problem: The study investigates the magnon band structure and topological properties of a magnetic system subjected to periodic lattice deformations and Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya (DM) interactions.
main_result: The lattice deformation enriches the band structure, leading to rich topological features, particularly in the ferromagnetic coupling case where Chern numbers can reach $\pm 2$.
method: The analysis employs linear spin-wave theory via the Holstein-Primakoff transformation, followed by diagonalizing the resulting bosonic Hamiltonian in momentum space to calculate topological invariants like the Chern number.
model_or_system: The system is a Heisenberg model with DM interaction defined on a Kagome lattice. The model is analyzed on a periodically deformed lattice, which modifies the exchange and DM couplings.
key_observables: Magnon band structure, Chern numbers ($C_n$), and the presence/closing of band gaps.
important_parameters: The nature of coupling (anti-ferromagnetic vs. ferromagnetic), the strength of the DM interaction ($D_z/J$), and the strength of the lattice deformation ($C$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on linear spin-wave theory and a $1/S$ expansion, which are approximations valid under certain conditions.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate band structures comparing undeformed vs. deformed lattices, and show the dependence of Chern numbers and band gaps on the deformation strength and DM interaction strength.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the Hamiltonian, first establishing the theoretical framework using spin-wave theory. It then compares the topological properties in the anti-ferromagnetic (trivial) versus ferromagnetic (rich) regimes, demonstrating how lattice deformation drives topological phase transitions.
why_interesting: The explicit connection between geometric deformation, magnetic interactions, and the emergence of non-trivial topology (quantized Chern numbers) provides a powerful mechanism for engineering exotic quantum states in condensed matter magnets.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
